rationale.R1.1: The broad delegation invites assessment of the paper excerpt's thesis framing, contribution claim, and conceptual setup.
rationale.R1.2: A general judgment on the excerpt centrally depends on checking whether the stated method description and argument chain are internally coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The excerpt includes empirical and design-effect claims, so assessing whether those claims match the reported evidence is a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact makes an explicit first-application and departure-from-prior-work claim, making novelty assessment central to the requested reaction.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of a vague request must preserve uncertainty about the intended depth and type of feedback.
rationale.RX.2: The response must avoid inflating the delegation into responsibilities not supported by the wording or artifact.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projection only and not perform the requested evaluation of the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid enabling misleading research feedback by marking risks such as overclaiming and scope limits.
rationale.RX.5: The rationale should remain traceable to the delegation wording and visible features of the artifact.
